```{r MIUKMI}
  M9  = c("loadings", SCALAR_PARAM, "residuals", "lv.variances", "lv.covariances"),
  M10 = c("loadings", SCALAR_PARAM, "means"),
  M11 = c("loadings", SCALAR_PARAM, "residuals", "means"),
  M12 = c("loadings", SCALAR_PARAM, "lv.variances", "lv.covariances", "means"),
  M13 = c("loadings", SCALAR_PARAM, "residuals", "lv.variances", "lv.covariances", "means")
)

epq_model_partial_uk    <- setNames(vector("list", 13), paste0("M", 1:13))
epq_model_extra_args_uk <- setNames(vector("list", 13), paste0("M", 1:13))

`%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Fitting function
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fit_epq_language_M_uk <- function(id) {
  base_args <- list(
    model            = epq_invariance_model_uk,
    data             = epq_language_data_uk,
    group            = "Language",
    ordered          = epq_ordered_items,
    estimator        = "WLSMV",
    parameterization = "theta",
    std.lv           = TRUE,
    meanstructure    = TRUE,
    group.equal      = epq_marsh13_equal_uk[[id]],
    group.partial    = epq_model_partial_uk[[id]]
  )

  args <- modifyList(base_args, epq_model_extra_args_uk[[id]] %||% list())
  do.call(lavaan::cfa, args)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Fit all 13 models with progress bar (error-safe)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
model_ids_uk <- paste0("M", 1:13)

epq_language_models_uk <- setNames(vector("list", length(model_ids_uk)), model_ids_uk)

epq_language_fit_status_uk <- tibble::tibble(
  Model         = model_ids_uk,
  fitted        = FALSE,
  converged     = NA,
  error_message = NA_character_
)

pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = length(model_ids_uk), style = 3)

for (i in seq_along(model_ids_uk)) {
  id <- model_ids_uk[i]
  cat(sprintf("\nFitting EPQ UK Language %s ...\n", id))

  fit_obj <- tryCatch(
    fit_epq_language_M_uk(id),
    error   = function(e) e,
    warning = function(w) w
  )

  if (inherits(fit_obj, "error")) {
    epq_language_models_uk[[id]]                <- NULL
    epq_language_fit_status_uk$fitted[i]        <- FALSE
    epq_language_fit_status_uk$converged[i]     <- NA
    epq_language_fit_status_uk$error_message[i] <- conditionMessage(fit_obj)
  } else {
    epq_language_models_uk[[id]]            <- fit_obj
    epq_language_fit_status_uk$fitted[i]    <- TRUE
    epq_language_fit_status_uk$converged[i] <- tryCatch(
      lavaan::lavInspect(fit_obj, "converged"),
      error = function(e) NA
    )
  }

  setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
}
close(pb)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Save model objects + fit status as separate UK files
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# saveRDS(
#   epq_language_models_uk,
#   "outputs/Language Invariance/EYP_language44_UK_models.rds"
# )
# 
# saveRDS(
#   epq_language_fit_status_uk,
#   "outputs/Language Invariance/EYP_language44_UK_fit_status.rds"
# )

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_fit_status_uk,
  "outputs/Language Invariance/EYP_language44_UK_fit_status.csv"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Helper: pick robust fit index with fallback
# ------------------------------------------------------------
.pick <- function(fm, primary, fallback = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(fm[[primary]]) && !is.na(fm[[primary]])) {
    return(unname(fm[[primary]]))
  }
  if (!is.null(fallback) && !is.null(fm[[fallback]]) && !is.na(fm[[fallback]])) {
    return(unname(fm[[fallback]]))
  }
  NA_real_
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Extract fit indices
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_fit_tbl_uk <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(model_ids_uk, function(id) {
  fit <- epq_language_models_uk[[id]]

  if (is.null(fit)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Model      = id,
      converged  = NA,
      Chi_Square = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      CFI        = NA_real_,
      TLI        = NA_real_,
      RMSEA      = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_L = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_U = NA_real_,
      SRMR       = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  conv <- tryCatch(lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "converged"), error = function(e) NA)
  fm   <- tryCatch(lavaan::fitMeasures(fit), error = function(e) NULL)

  if (is.null(fm)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      Model      = id,
      converged  = conv,
      Chi_Square = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      CFI        = NA_real_,
      TLI        = NA_real_,
      RMSEA      = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_L = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_90_U = NA_real_,
      SRMR       = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  tibble::tibble(
    Model      = id,
    converged  = conv,
    Chi_Square = .pick(fm, "chisq.scaled",          "chisq"),
    df         = .pick(fm, "df.scaled",             "df"),
    CFI        = .pick(fm, "cfi.scaled",            "cfi"),
    TLI        = .pick(fm, "tli.scaled",            "tli"),
    RMSEA      = .pick(fm, "rmsea.scaled",          "rmsea"),
    RMSEA_90_L = .pick(fm, "rmsea.ci.lower.scaled", "rmsea.ci.lower"),
    RMSEA_90_U = .pick(fm, "rmsea.ci.upper.scaled", "rmsea.ci.upper"),
    SRMR       = .pick(fm, "srmr",                  "srmr_bentler")
  )
})) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = model_ids_uk)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Model)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Sequential LRT
# ------------------------------------------------------------
safe_LRT <- function(prev, curr) {
  if (is.null(prev) || is.null(curr)) {
    return(c(chisq_diff = NA_real_, df_diff = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  }

  tab <- tryCatch(lavaan::lavTestLRT(prev, curr), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(tab)) {
    return(c(chisq_diff = NA_real_, df_diff = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  }

  tab <- as.data.frame(tab)
  i   <- nrow(tab)

  chisq <- if ("Chisq diff" %in% names(tab)) tab[["Chisq diff"]][i] else NA_real_
  df    <- if ("Df diff"    %in% names(tab)) tab[["Df diff"]][i]    else NA_real_
  p     <- if ("Pr(>Chisq)" %in% names(tab)) tab[["Pr(>Chisq)"]][i] else NA_real_

  c(
    chisq_diff = as.numeric(chisq),
    df_diff    = as.numeric(df),
    p          = as.numeric(p)
  )
}

epq_language_diff_tbl_uk <- tibble::tibble(
  Model      = model_ids_uk,
  chisq_diff = NA_real_,
  df_diff    = NA_real_,
  p          = NA_real_
)

for (i in 2:length(model_ids_uk)) {
  d <- safe_LRT(
    epq_language_models_uk[[model_ids_uk[i - 1]]],
    epq_language_models_uk[[model_ids_uk[i]]]
  )
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$chisq_diff[i] <- d[["chisq_diff"]]
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$df_diff[i]    <- d[["df_diff"]]
  epq_language_diff_tbl_uk$p[i]          <- d[["p"]]
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. Combined table with delta criteria
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_invariance_table_uk <- epq_language_fit_tbl_uk %>%
  dplyr::left_join(epq_language_diff_tbl_uk, by = "Model") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dCFI             = CFI   - dplyr::lag(CFI),
    dTLI             = TLI   - dplyr::lag(TLI),
    dRMSEA           = RMSEA - dplyr::lag(RMSEA),
    CFI_invariance   = dCFI   >= -0.01,
    RMSEA_invariance = dRMSEA <=  0.01
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 13. Pretty table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk <- epq_language_invariance_table_uk %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Model,
    `Chi-Square`            = Chi_Square,
    df,
    CFI,
    TLI,
    RMSEA,
    `RMSEA 90% Lower CI`    = RMSEA_90_L,
    `RMSEA 90% Upper CI`    = RMSEA_90_U,
    `Chi-Square Difference` = chisq_diff,
    `df difference`         = df_diff,
    p,
    CFI_diff                = dCFI,
    TLI_diff                = dTLI,
    RMSEA_diff              = dRMSEA,
    CFI_invariance,
    RMSEA_invariance,
    converged
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 14. Formatting helpers
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fmt2 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf("%.2f", x))
fmt3 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf("%.3f", x))

apa_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p), "",
    ifelse(p < .001, "p < .001",
           sub("^0", "", sprintf("%.3f", p)))
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 15. Final table formatter
# ------------------------------------------------------------
prep_invariance_tbl <- function(df, model_col = "Model") {
  m13_labels <- c(
    M1  = "M1 (Configural)",
    M2  = "M2 (Metric)",
    M3  = "M3 (Metric + Residuals)",
    M4  = "M4 (Metric + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M5  = "M5 (Scalar)",
    M6  = "M6 (Metric + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M7  = "M7 (Scalar + Residuals)",
    M8  = "M8 (Scalar + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M9  = "M9 (Scalar + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov)",
    M10 = "M10 (Scalar + Means)",
    M11 = "M11 (Scalar + Residuals + Means)",
    M12 = "M12 (Scalar + Factor Var/Cov + Means)",
    M13 = "M13 (Scalar + Residuals + Factor Var/Cov + Means)"
  )

  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model_disp = if ("Model_Label" %in% names(.)) .data[["Model_Label"]] else .data[[model_col]],
      Model_disp = dplyr::recode(
        as.character(Model_disp),
        !!!as.list(m13_labels),
        .default = as.character(Model_disp)
      ),
      RMSEA_CI = ifelse(
        is.na(.data[["RMSEA 90% Lower CI"]]) | is.na(.data[["RMSEA 90% Upper CI"]]),
        "",
        paste0(
          "[", fmt3(.data[["RMSEA 90% Lower CI"]]),
          ", ", fmt3(.data[["RMSEA 90% Upper CI"]]), "]"
        )
      )
    )

  df %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Model          = Model_disp,
      `χ²`           = fmt2(.data[["Chi-Square"]]),
      df             = ifelse(is.na(.data[["df"]]), "",
                              as.character(as.integer(.data[["df"]]))),
      CFI            = fmt3(.data[["CFI"]]),
      TLI            = fmt3(.data[["TLI"]]),
      RMSEA          = fmt3(.data[["RMSEA"]]),
      `RMSEA 90% CI` = RMSEA_CI,
      `Δχ²`          = fmt2(.data[["Chi-Square Difference"]]),
      `Δdf`          = ifelse(is.na(.data[["df difference"]]), "",
                              as.character(as.integer(.data[["df difference"]]))),
      p              = apa_p(.data[["p"]]),
      `ΔCFI`         = fmt3(.data[["CFI_diff"]]),
      `ΔTLI`         = fmt3(.data[["TLI_diff"]]),
      `ΔRMSEA`       = fmt3(.data[["RMSEA_diff"]]),
      CFI_inv        = ifelse(is.na(.data[["CFI_invariance"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["CFI_invariance"]], "Yes", "No")),
      RMSEA_inv      = ifelse(is.na(.data[["RMSEA_invariance"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["RMSEA_invariance"]], "Yes", "No")),
      Converged      = ifelse(is.na(.data[["converged"]]), "",
                              ifelse(.data[["converged"]], "Yes", "No"))
    )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 16. Create APA-ready table
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_language44_invariance_apa_uk <- prep_invariance_tbl(epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 17. Save all outputs as separate UK files
# ------------------------------------------------------------

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_fit_tbl_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_fit_table.csv"
)

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language_invariance_pretty_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_raw.csv"
)

readr::write_csv(
  epq_language44_invariance_apa_uk,
  "outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_APA.csv"
)


```


```{r}
epq_language_invariance_apa <- read_csv("outputs/tables/EYP_language44_UK_invariance_APA.csv")
papaja::apa_table(
  epq_language_invariance_apa,
  caption = "Measurement invariance (Language) results for the EPQ-RS-B.",
  note = "Δ values are relative to the immediately preceding model. * p < .05, ** p < .01, *** p < .001."
)
```

## EPQ-RS ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING ANALYSIS

```{r gpt2}
################################################################################
# EPQ-RS CROSS-CULTURAL ALIGNMENT BENCHMARKING
# R-only approximation to the logic used in:
# - McLarnon & Romero (2020)
# - Asparouhov & Muthén, Web Note 18
#
# What this script does:
#   1) Reads all archival UK-scored EPQ country files + Bangla epq_items
#   2) Keeps England as the exact reference file
#   3) Uses the common-item map
#   4) Estimates group-specific configural 1-factor CFAs for each scale
#      using categorical/probit-style ordered-item modeling in lavaan
#   5) Feeds configural loadings + thresholds into sirt alignment
#   6) Tries a FREE-like alignment first, then FIXED-like if needed
#   7) Re-expresses aligned means/SDs relative to England
#   8) Exports alignment effect sizes and R2 summaries
#
# Important:
#   - This is the closest R-only approximation.
#   - It is NOT the exact Mplus implementation from the paper.
################################################################################

# ---- 0. PACKAGES -------------------------------------------------------------

required_pkgs <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr", "stringr", "tibble",
  "readr", "ggplot2", "lavaan", "sirt"
)

invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
  }
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}))

# ---- 1. CONFIGURATION --------------------------------------------------------

ARCHIVAL_DIR   <- "EPQ- Invariance/Analysis-Files/UKscored/Total/Excel/"
OUT_DIR        <- "alignment_output_r_approx"
BANGLA_LABEL   <- "Bangladesh (Bangla)"
ENGLAND_FILE   <- "England-total sample UK scored EPQ item data.xls"

# Heuristic flags for larger residual non-invariance
LAMBDA_TOL <- 0.20
NU_TOL     <- 0.40

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ---- 2. VERIFY BANGLA INPUT --------------------------------------------------

if (!exists("epq_items")) {
  stop("Object `epq_items` was not found in the R environment.")
}
epq_items <- as.data.frame(epq_items)

# ---- 3. ITEM MAP -------------------------------------------------------------

common_item_map <- tibble::tribble(
  ~EYP,    ~archival_col, ~scale,
  "EYP1",  "N_3",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP3",  "E_5",  "Extraversion",
  "EYP4",  "L_13", "Lie",
  "EYP5",  "N_7",  "Neuroticism",
  "EYP6",  "P_6",  "Psychoticism",
  "EYP7",  "E_10", "Extraversion",
  "EYP8",  "L_8",  "Lie",
  "EYP9",  "N_15", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP10", "P_22", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP11", "E_17", "Extraversion",
  "EYP12", "L_16", "Lie",
  "EYP13", "N_19", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP15", "E_14", "Extraversion",
  "EYP16", "L_20", "Lie",
  "EYP17", "N_23", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP18", "P_37", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP19", "E_40", "Extraversion",
  "EYP20", "L_24", "Lie",
  "EYP21", "N_31", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP22", "P_43", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP23", "E_45", "Extraversion",
  "EYP24", "L_39", "Lie",
  "EYP25", "N_34", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP27", "E_21", "Extraversion",
  "EYP28", "P_53", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP29", "L_48", "Lie",
  "EYP30", "N_41", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP31", "P_67", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP32", "E_52", "Extraversion",
  "EYP33", "L_51", "Lie",
  "EYP34", "N_72", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP35", "P_71", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP36", "E_82", "Extraversion",
  "EYP37", "L_59", "Lie",
  "EYP38", "N_75", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP39", "P_83", "Psychoticism",
  "EYP40", "L_63", "Lie",
  "EYP41", "E_42", "Extraversion",
  "EYP42", "N_77", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP44", "E_86", "Extraversion",
  "EYP45", "L_78", "Lie",
  "EYP46", "N_27", "Neuroticism",
  "EYP47", "L_85", "Lie",
  "EYP48", "E_70", "Extraversion"
)

epq_common_item_sets <- list(
  Psychoticism = c("EYP6","EYP10","EYP18","EYP22","EYP28","EYP31","EYP35","EYP39"),
  Extraversion = c("EYP3","EYP7","EYP11","EYP15","EYP19","EYP23","EYP27","EYP32",
                   "EYP36","EYP41","EYP44","EYP48"),
  Neuroticism  = c("EYP1","EYP5","EYP9","EYP13","EYP17","EYP21","EYP25","EYP30",
                   "EYP34","EYP38","EYP42","EYP46"),
  Lie          = c("EYP4","EYP8","EYP12","EYP16","EYP20","EYP24","EYP29","EYP33",
                   "EYP37","EYP40","EYP45","EYP47")
)

archival_to_eyp <- setNames(common_item_map$EYP, common_item_map$archival_col)

# ---- 4. HELPERS --------------------------------------------------------------

infer_country_from_filename <- function(path) {
  x <- basename(path)
  x <- stringr::str_remove(x, "\\.[^.]+$")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "_", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, "-", " ")
  x <- stringr::str_replace(
    x,
    regex("total sample uk scored epq item data", ignore_case = TRUE),
    ""
  )
  stringr::str_squish(x)
}

normalize_binary <- function(x) {
  if (is.factor(x)) x <- as.character(x)
  if (is.logical(x)) x <- as.integer(x)

  if (is.character(x)) {
    x_clean <- stringr::str_to_lower(stringr::str_trim(x))
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x_clean %in% c("yes", "y", "1", "true", "t") ~ 1,
      x_clean %in% c("no",  "n", "0", "false", "f") ~ 0,
      x_clean %in% c("", "na", "n/a", ".", "missing") ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_clean))
    )
  } else {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  }

  vals <- sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x)))

  # Common archival coding: 1 = No, 3 = Yes, 2 = missing
  if (all(vals %in% c(1, 2, 3))) {
    x <- dplyr::case_when(
      x == 1 ~ 0,
      x == 3 ~ 1,
      x == 2 ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE   ~ x
    )
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 2))) {
    x <- x - 1
    return(x)
  }

  if (identical(vals, c(1, 3))) {
    x <- ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, ifelse(x == 3, 1, 0))
    return(x)
  }

  bad <- setdiff(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(x))), c(0, 1))
  if (length(bad) > 0) {
    warning("Non-binary values detected: ", paste(bad, collapse = ", "))
  }

  x
}

coerce_binary_df <- function(df) {
  as.data.frame(lapply(df, normalize_binary))
}

read_archival_country <- function(path) {
  raw <- readxl::read_excel(path)
  raw <- as.data.frame(raw)
  names(raw) <- stringr::str_squish(names(raw))

  needed <- common_item_map$archival_col
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(raw))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing archival columns in ", basename(path), ": ",
         paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- raw[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  names(dat) <- archival_to_eyp[names(dat)]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = infer_country_from_filename(path),
    source_file = path,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

read_bangla_country <- function(epq_items_df) {
  needed <- common_item_map$EYP
  missing_cols <- setdiff(needed, names(epq_items_df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Bangla epq_items is missing: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }

  dat <- epq_items_df[, needed, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- coerce_binary_df(dat)

  list(
    country = BANGLA_LABEL,
    source_file = NA_character_,
    data = dat,
    n_raw = nrow(dat)
  )
}

fit_configural_group_cfa <- function(df, items, scale_name, country_name) {
  dat <- df[, items, drop = FALSE]
  dat <- dat[stats::complete.cases(dat), , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(dat) == 0) {
    stop("No complete cases for ", scale_name, " in ", country_name)
  }

  bad_items <- names(which(vapply(dat, function(x) length(unique(stats::na.omit(x))) < 2, logical(1))))
  if (length(bad_items) > 0) {
    stop(
      "Zero-variance item(s) in ", country_name, " for ", scale_name, ": ",
      paste(bad_items, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  model_syntax <- paste0("F =~ ", paste(items, collapse = " + "))

  fit <- lavaan::cfa(
    model = model_syntax,
    data = dat,
    ordered = items,
    estimator = "WLSMV",
    parameterization = "theta",
    std.lv = TRUE
  )

  if (!lavaan::inspect(fit, "converged")) {
    stop("lavaan CFA did not converge for ", scale_name, " in ", country_name)
  }

  pe <- lavaan::parameterEstimates(fit, standardized = FALSE)

  loading_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "=~", lhs == "F") |>
    dplyr::select(rhs, est)

  lambda <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  lambda[loading_df$rhs] <- loading_df$est

  thr_df <- pe |>
    dplyr::filter(op == "|") |>
    dplyr::select(lhs, est) |>
    dplyr::distinct(lhs, .keep_all = TRUE)

  tau <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(items)), items)
  common_items <- intersect(items, thr_df$lhs)
  tau[common_items] <- thr_df$est[match(common_items, thr_df$lhs)]

  # For binary categorical CFA, use intercept-like parameter nu = -tau
  nu <- -tau

  list(
    fit = fit,
    n_complete = nrow(dat),
    lambda = lambda,
    nu = nu
  )
}

extract_alignment_es_r2 <- function(alignment_obj, scale_name, chosen_method) {
  es_obj <- alignment_obj$es.invariance

  # Robust coercion to matrix/data.frame
  es_df <- as.data.frame(es_obj)

  # If rownames are missing, try to infer them
  if (is.null(rownames(es_df)) || all(rownames(es_df) == as.character(seq_len(nrow(es_df))))) {
    if (nrow(es_df) == 3) {
      rownames(es_df) <- c("R2", "sqrtU2", "rbar")
    }
  }

  es_df$metric <- rownames(es_df)
  rownames(es_df) <- NULL

  # Normalize likely column names
  names(es_df) <- gsub("\\.+", "_", names(es_df))
  names(es_df) <- tolower(names(es_df))

  # Try to map columns to expected parameters
  col_map <- names(es_df)
  col_map[col_map %in% c("loadings", "loading", "lambda")] <- "loading"
  col_map[col_map %in% c("intercepts", "intercept", "nu", "threshold", "thresholds")] <- "threshold/intercept"
  names(es_df) <- col_map

  # Keep only known columns plus metric
  keep_cols <- intersect(names(es_df), c("metric", "loading", "threshold/intercept"))
  es_df <- es_df[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]

  es_long <- es_df |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = -metric,
      names_to = "parameter",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method,
      .before = 1
    )

  r2_tbl <- es_long |>
    dplyr::filter(metric == "R2") |>
    dplyr::select(scale, method, parameter, value) |>
    dplyr::rename(R2 = value)

  r2_summary <- r2_tbl |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = parameter,
      values_from = R2
    )

  # Ensure columns exist
  if (!"loading" %in% names(r2_summary)) {
    r2_summary$loading <- NA_real_
  }
  if (!"threshold/intercept" %in% names(r2_summary)) {
    r2_summary$`threshold/intercept` <- NA_real_
  }

  r2_summary <- r2_summary |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      min_R2 = pmin(loading, `threshold/intercept`, na.rm = TRUE),
      acceptable_by_R2_90 = dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(loading) &
          !is.na(`threshold/intercept`) &
          loading >= .90 &
          `threshold/intercept` >= .90,
        TRUE, FALSE,
        missing = FALSE
      )
    )

  list(
    es_long = es_long,
    r2_tbl = r2_tbl,
    r2_summary = r2_summary
  )
}

run_alignment_one_scale <- function(scale_name, all_groups, group_key) {
  message("\n==============================")
  message("Running scale: ", scale_name)
  message("==============================")

  items <- epq_common_item_sets[[scale_name]]

  config_est <- purrr::map(
    all_groups,
    ~ fit_configural_group_cfa(
      df = .x$data,
      items = items,
      scale_name = scale_name,
      country_name = .x$country
    )
  )

  lambda <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "lambda"))
  nu     <- do.call(rbind, purrr::map(config_est, "nu"))
  n_vec  <- purrr::map_int(config_est, "n_complete")

  rownames(lambda) <- group_key$country
  rownames(nu)     <- group_key$country
  colnames(lambda) <- items
  colnames(nu)     <- items

  # Weighting based on group sample sizes
  wgt <- matrix(sqrt(n_vec), nrow = length(n_vec), ncol = length(items))
  colnames(wgt) <- items
  rownames(wgt) <- group_key$country

  # FREE-like first
  free_res <- tryCatch(
    sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = FALSE,
      meth = 4
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  chosen <- free_res
  chosen_method <- "FREE-like (sirt meth=4)"
  fallback_used <- FALSE

  if (is.null(chosen)) {
    fallback_used <- TRUE
  } else {
    bad_pars <- any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$alpha0)) ||
      any(!is.finite(chosen$pars$psi0)) ||
      any(chosen$pars$psi0 <= 0)

    if (bad_pars) fallback_used <- TRUE
  }

  if (fallback_used) {
    chosen <- sirt::invariance.alignment(
      lambda = lambda,
      nu = nu,
      wgt = wgt,
      align.scale = c(1, 1),
      align.pow = c(.5, .5),
      center = FALSE,
      fixed = TRUE,
      meth = 3
    )
    chosen_method <- "FIXED-like (sirt meth=3)"
  }

  pars <- as.data.frame(chosen$pars)
  pars$country <- group_key$country
  pars$scale <- scale_name
  pars$method <- chosen_method
  pars$n_complete <- n_vec

  # Re-express on England-referenced metric
  eng_idx <- which(group_key$is_reference)
  alpha_eng <- pars$alpha0[eng_idx]
  psi_eng   <- pars$psi0[eng_idx]

  pars$mean_eng_ref     <- (pars$alpha0 - alpha_eng) / psi_eng
  pars$sd_eng_ref       <- pars$psi0 / psi_eng
  pars$variance_eng_ref <- pars$sd_eng_ref^2

  # Alignment effect sizes and R2
  es_r2_out <- extract_alignment_es_r2(chosen, scale_name, chosen_method)
  es         <- es_r2_out$es_long
  r2_tbl     <- es_r2_out$r2_tbl
  r2_summary <- es_r2_out$r2_summary

  # Residual non-invariance tables
  lambda_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$lambda.resid)
  lambda_resid$country <- rownames(lambda_resid)

  nu_resid <- as.data.frame(chosen$nu.resid)
  nu_resid$country <- rownames(nu_resid)

  lambda_noninv <- lambda_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "loading",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > LAMBDA_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  nu_noninv <- nu_resid |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(-country, names_to = "item", values_to = "resid") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      parameter = "threshold/intercept",
      abs_resid = abs(resid),
      flagged = abs_resid > NU_TOL,
      scale = scale_name,
      method = chosen_method
    )

  noninv <- dplyr::bind_rows(lambda_noninv, nu_noninv)

  config_lambda <- as.data.frame(lambda)
  config_lambda$country <- rownames(config_lambda)
  config_lambda$scale <- scale_name

  config_nu <- as.data.frame(nu)
  config_nu$country <- rownames(config_nu)
  config_nu$scale <- scale_name

  list(
    scale = scale_name,
    items = items,
    method = chosen_method,
    config_est = config_est,
    config_lambda = config_lambda,
    config_nu = config_nu,
    alignment = chosen,
    pars = pars,
    es = es,
    r2_tbl = r2_tbl,
    r2_summary = r2_summary,
    noninv = noninv
  )
}

# ---- 5. READ GROUPS ----------------------------------------------------------

archival_files <- list.files(
  ARCHIVAL_DIR,
  pattern = "\\.(xls|xlsx)$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

if (length(archival_files) == 0) {
  stop("No archival Excel files found in: ", ARCHIVAL_DIR)
}

archival_groups <- purrr::map(archival_files, read_archival_country)
bangla_group    <- read_bangla_country(epq_items)

all_groups <- c(archival_groups, list(bangla_group))

source_files <- purrr::map_chr(
  all_groups,
  ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
)

eng_idx <- which(source_files == ENGLAND_FILE)

if (length(eng_idx) == 0) {
  stop("England reference file was not found. Expected file name: ", ENGLAND_FILE)
}

reorder_idx <- c(eng_idx[1], setdiff(seq_along(all_groups), eng_idx[1]))
all_groups  <- all_groups[reorder_idx]

group_key <- tibble::tibble(
  code = seq_along(all_groups),
  country = purrr::map_chr(all_groups, "country"),
  n_raw = purrr::map_int(all_groups, "n_raw"),
  source_file = purrr::map_chr(
    all_groups,
    ~ if (is.null(.x$source_file) || all(is.na(.x$source_file))) "" else basename(.x$source_file)
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    is_reference = source_file == ENGLAND_FILE
  )

readr::write_csv(group_key, file.path(OUT_DIR, "country_key.csv"))

message(
  "England reference group: ",
  group_key$country[group_key$is_reference][1],
  " [", group_key$source_file[group_key$is_reference][1], "]"
)

# ---- 6. RUN ALIGNMENT --------------------------------------------------------

alignment_results <- purrr::map(
  names(epq_common_item_sets),
  ~ run_alignment_one_scale(.x, all_groups = all_groups, group_key = group_key)
)
names(alignment_results) <- names(epq_common_item_sets)

# ---- 7. EXPORT RESULTS -------------------------------------------------------

aligned_means <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "pars"))
alignment_es  <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "es"))
alignment_r2  <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "r2_tbl"))
r2_summary    <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "r2_summary"))
noninv_tbl    <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "noninv"))
config_lambda <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_lambda"))
config_nu     <- dplyr::bind_rows(purrr::map(alignment_results, "config_nu"))

readr::write_csv(aligned_means, file.path(OUT_DIR, "aligned_factor_means_variances.csv"))
readr::write_csv(alignment_es,  file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_effect_sizes.csv"))
readr::write_csv(alignment_r2,  file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_r2_long.csv"))
readr::write_csv(r2_summary,    file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_r2_summary.csv"))
readr::write_csv(noninv_tbl,    file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariant_params.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_lambda, file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_loadings.csv"))
readr::write_csv(config_nu,     file.path(OUT_DIR, "configural_nu.csv"))

# ---- 8. BANGLA PERCENTILE ----------------------------------------------------

bangla_percentile <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    z_against_groups = as.numeric(scale(mean_eng_ref)),
    percentile = dplyr::percent_rank(mean_eng_ref) * 100
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(country == BANGLA_LABEL)

readr::write_csv(bangla_percentile, file.path(OUT_DIR, "bangla_percentile.csv"))

# ---- 9. SUMMARIES ------------------------------------------------------------

noninv_summary <- noninv_tbl |>
  dplyr::group_by(scale, parameter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    flagged_n = sum(flagged, na.rm = TRUE),
    total = dplyr::n(),
    pct_flagged = 100 * flagged_n / total,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

readr::write_csv(noninv_summary, file.path(OUT_DIR, "noninvariance_summary.csv"))

# ---- 10. PLOT ----------------------------------------------------------------

plot_df <- aligned_means |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    is_bangla = country == BANGLA_LABEL,
    scale = factor(scale, levels = c("Psychoticism", "Extraversion", "Neuroticism", "Lie"))
  )

p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  plot_df,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = mean_eng_ref,
    y = reorder(country, mean_eng_ref),
    colour = is_bangla,
    size = is_bangla
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ scale, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "grey60", "TRUE" = "#C0392B"),
    labels = c("Other group", BANGLA_LABEL),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 1.8, "TRUE" = 4), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Aligned factor means relative to England",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "R-only approximation to McLarnon/Web Note 18 logic; England reference file = ",
      ENGLAND_FILE
    ),
    x = "Aligned latent mean (England = 0)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
  )

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(OUT_DIR, "benchmark_plot.pdf"),
  plot = p,
  width = 12,
  height = max(10, 0.22 * dplyr::n_distinct(plot_df$country))
)

# ---- 11. SAVE WORKSPACE ------------------------------------------------------

saveRDS(
  list(
    group_key = group_key,
    alignment_results = alignment_results,
    aligned_means = aligned_means,
    alignment_es = alignment_es,
    alignment_r2 = alignment_r2,
    r2_summary = r2_summary,
    noninv_tbl = noninv_tbl,
    noninv_summary = noninv_summary,
    bangla_percentile = bangla_percentile
  ),
  file = file.path(OUT_DIR, "alignment_workspace.rds")
)

print(r2_summary)

message("\nDone. Output folder: ", normalizePath(OUT_DIR, winslash = "/"))
```

## Item Response Theory

```{r unidimentionality, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(lavaan)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Define item sets for the four EPQ-RS scales
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_scale_items <- list(
  Psychoticism = c("EYP10","EYP14","EYP22","EYP31","EYP39","EYP2","EYP6","EYP18","EYP26","EYP28","EYP35","EYP43"),
  Extraversion = c("EYP3","EYP7","EYP11","EYP15","EYP19","EYP23","EYP32","EYP36","EYP44","EYP48","EYP27","EYP41"),
  Neuroticism  = c("EYP1","EYP5","EYP9","EYP13","EYP17","EYP21","EYP25","EYP30","EYP34","EYP38","EYP42","EYP46"),
  Lie          = c("EYP4","EYP16","EYP45","EYP8","EYP12","EYP20","EYP24","EYP29","EYP33","EYP37","EYP40","EYP47")
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Function to fit 1-factor CFA for a scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fit_epq_unidim_cfa <- function(data, items, factor_name) {
  
  dat <- epq_items %>%
    select(all_of(items)) %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), as.ordered))
  
  # Drop rows with all missing on that scale
  dat <- dat[rowSums(!is.na(dat)) > 0, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # 1-factor model
  model_syntax <- paste0(
    factor_name, " =~ ", paste(items, collapse = " + ")
  )
  
  fit <- lavaan::cfa(
    model = model_syntax,
    data = dat,
    ordered = items,
    estimator = "WLSMV",
    parameterization = "theta",
    std.lv = TRUE,
    missing = "pairwise"
  )
  
  fit
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Fit all four 1-factor CFAs
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epq_cfa_1f <- imap(epq_scale_items, ~ fit_epq_unidim_cfa(
  data = epq_items,
  items = .x,
  factor_name = .y
))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Extract fit indices
# ------------------------------------------------------------


fmt2 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf("%.2f", x))

fmt3_apa <- function(x) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(x),
    "",
    sub("^0", "", sprintf("%.3f", x))
  )
}

fmt_p_apa <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    is.na(p)   ~ "",
    p < .001   ~ "***",
    p < .01    ~ "**",
    p < .05    ~ "*",
    TRUE       ~ ""
  )
}

fmt_chisq_df_apa <- function(chisq, df, p) {
  paste0(
    fmt2(chisq),
    " (",
    ifelse(is.na(df), "", as.integer(round(df))),
    ")",
    fmt_p_apa(p)
  )
}




epq_cfa_fit_table <- imap_dfr(epq_cfa_1f, function(fit, scale_name) {
  tibble(
    Scale            = scale_name,
    chisq.scaled     = fitMeasures(fit, "chisq.scaled"),
    df.scaled        = fitMeasures(fit, "df.scaled"),
    p.scaled         = fitMeasures(fit, "pvalue.scaled"),
    cfi.scaled       = fitMeasures(fit, "cfi.scaled"),
    tli.scaled       = fitMeasures(fit, "tli.scaled"),
    rmsea.scaled     = fitMeasures(fit, "rmsea.scaled"),
    rmsea.ci.lower   = fitMeasures(fit, "rmsea.ci.lower.scaled"),
    rmsea.ci.upper   = fitMeasures(fit, "rmsea.ci.upper.scaled"),
    srmr             = fitMeasures(fit, "srmr")
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(
    `χ² (df)`      = fmt_chisq_df_apa(chisq.scaled, df.scaled, p.scaled),
    `RMSEA 90% CI` = paste0(fmt3_apa(rmsea.ci.lower), "-", fmt3_apa(rmsea.ci.upper))
  ) %>%
  select(
    Scale,
    `χ² (df)`,
    cfi.scaled,
    tli.scaled,
    rmsea.scaled,
    `RMSEA 90% CI`,
    srmr
  )

epq_cfa_fit_table

# Psychoticism needs modification
mi_psy_clean <- modindices(epq_cfa_1f$Psychoticism, sort. = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  arrange(desc(mi)) %>%
  select(lhs, op, rhs, mi, epc, sepc.lv, sepc.all)

mi_psy_clean


# Refiting psychotocism
psychoticism_mod_1 <- '
Psychoticism =~ EYP10 + EYP14 + EYP22 + EYP31 + EYP39 + EYP2 +
                 EYP6 + EYP18 + EYP26 + EYP28 + EYP35 + EYP43

EYP26 ~~ EYP43
EYP2 ~~ EYP28
EYP28 ~~EYP35
EYP2 ~~ EYP18 
EYP2 ~~  EYP6
EYP14 ~~ EYP28
EYP22 ~~ EYP28
EYP14 ~~ EYP43
'

fit_psychoticism_mod_1 <- cfa(
  psychoticism_mod_1,
  data = epq_items,
  ordered = c("EYP10","EYP14","EYP22","EYP31","EYP39","EYP2",
              "EYP6","EYP18","EYP26","EYP28","EYP35","EYP43"),
  estimator = "WLSMV",
  parameterization = "theta",
  std.lv = TRUE,
  missing = "pairwise"
)

summary(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)
modindices(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, sort. = TRUE)



# Updating the model
psychoticism_mod_row <- tibble(
  Scale          = "Psychoticism (modified)",
  chisq.scaled   = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "chisq.scaled"),
  df.scaled      = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "df.scaled"),
  p.scaled       = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "pvalue.scaled"),
  cfi.scaled     = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "cfi.scaled"),
  tli.scaled     = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "tli.scaled"),
  rmsea.scaled   = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "rmsea.scaled"),
  rmsea.ci.lower = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "rmsea.ci.lower.scaled"),
  rmsea.ci.upper = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "rmsea.ci.upper.scaled"),
  srmr           = fitMeasures(fit_psychoticism_mod_1, "srmr")
) %>%
  mutate(
    `χ² (df)`      = fmt_chisq_df_apa(chisq.scaled, df.scaled, p.scaled),
    `RMSEA 90% CI` = paste0(fmt3_apa(rmsea.ci.lower), "-", fmt3_apa(rmsea.ci.upper))
  ) %>%
  select(
    Scale,
    `χ² (df)`,
    cfi.scaled,
    tli.scaled,
    rmsea.scaled,
    `RMSEA 90% CI`,
    srmr
  )

epq_cfa_fit_table_updated <- bind_rows(
  epq_cfa_fit_table,
  psychoticism_mod_row
) %>%
  mutate(
    Scale = factor(
      Scale,
      levels = c(
        "Extraversion",
        "Neuroticism",
        "Lie",
        "Psychoticism",
        "Psychoticism (modified)"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(Scale) %>%
  mutate(Scale = as.character(Scale))

epq_cfa_fit_table_updated
```

```{r}
papaja::apa_table(
  epq_cfa_fit_table_updated,
  caption = "Scaled fit indices for one-factor CFA models of the EPQ-RS-B subscales.",
  note = "Values in the chi-square column are scaled chi-square statistics. RMSEA CI = 90% confidence interval for RMSEA. * p < .05. ** p < .01. *** p < .001.",
  align = c("l", "l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
  escape = FALSE
)
```

```{r epq-irt, include=FALSE}
epq_irt_pars <- list()
epq_irt_tif  <- list()
epq_irt_fit  <- list()
epq_irt_rel  <- list()
epq_irt_conv <- list()
epq_irt_disc <- list()
epq_irt_m2   <- list()
epq_models    <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_item_sets)) {
  dat <- epq_items[, epq_item_sets[[sc]], drop = FALSE]
  
  mod <- mirt::mirt(
    dat,
    1,
    itemtype = "2PL",
    method = "MHRM",
    verbose = FALSE
  )
  
  epq_models[[sc]] <- mod
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # Model convergence confirmation
  # --------------------------------------------------
  epq_irt_conv[[sc]] <- tibble::tibble(
    Scale              = sc,
    converged          = mirt::extract.mirt(mod, "converged"),
    iterations         = mirt::extract.mirt(mod, "iterations"),
    second_order_test  = mirt::extract.mirt(mod, "secondordertest"),
    sem_info_converged = mirt::extract.mirt(mod, "SEMconv"),
    logLik             = mirt::extract.mirt(mod, "logLik")
  )
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # M2 model fit
  # --------------------------------------------------
  epq_irt_m2[[sc]] <- tryCatch({
    m2_out <- as.data.frame(
      mirt::M2(mod, type = "M2", calcNull = TRUE, CI = 0.90)
    )
    
    # standardize possible column names just in case
    if ("M2*" %in% names(m2_out)) names(m2_out)[names(m2_out) == "M2*"] <- "M2"
    if ("C2"  %in% names(m2_out)) names(m2_out)[names(m2_out) == "C2"]  <- "M2"
    
    tibble::as_tibble(m2_out) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(Scale = sc, .before = 1)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    tibble::tibble(
      Scale    = sc,
      M2       = NA_real_,
      df       = NA_real_,
      p        = NA_real_,
      RMSEA    = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_5  = NA_real_,
      RMSEA_95 = NA_real_,
      SRMSR    = NA_real_,
      TLI      = NA_real_,
      CFI      = NA_real_,
      M2_error = conditionMessage(e)
    )
  })
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # Item parameters
  # --------------------------------------------------
  epq_irt_pars[[sc]] <- extract_mirt_pars(mod, sc)
  epq_irt_tif[[sc]]  <- make_tif_summary(mod, sc)
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # Item fit
  # --------------------------------------------------
  epq_irt_fit[[sc]] <- tryCatch({
    sx2 <- mirt::itemfit(mod, fit_stats = "S_X2") |>
      as.data.frame() |>
      tibble::rownames_to_column("Item")
    
    inout <- mirt::itemfit(mod, fit_stats = "infit") |>
      as.data.frame() |>
      tibble::rownames_to_column("Item")
    
    dplyr::left_join(sx2, inout, by = "Item") |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        Scale = sc,
        infit_flag  = ifelse(infit < 0.5 | infit > 1.5, "misfit", ""),
        outfit_flag = ifelse(outfit < 0.5 | outfit > 1.5, "misfit", ""),
        .before = 1
      )
  },
  error = function(e) tibble::tibble(
    Scale = sc, Item = names(dat),
    S_X2 = NA_real_, df = NA_real_, p = NA_real_,
    infit = NA_real_, outfit = NA_real_,
    infit_flag = NA_character_, outfit_flag = NA_character_
  ))
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # Marginal and empirical reliability
  # --------------------------------------------------
  marginal <- mirt::marginal_rxx(mod)
  
  theta_se <- mirt::fscores(mod, full.scores.SE = TRUE)
  se_vals  <- theta_se[, 2]
  emp_rel  <- 1 - mean(se_vals^2, na.rm = TRUE) / var(theta_se[, 1], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  epq_irt_rel[[sc]] <- tibble::tibble(
    Scale                 = sc,
    Marginal_Reliability  = round(marginal, 3),
    Empirical_Reliability = round(emp_rel, 3)
  )
  
  # --------------------------------------------------
  # Discrimination categorization
  # --------------------------------------------------
  item_coef <- as.data.frame(mirt::coef(mod, IRTpars = TRUE, simplify = TRUE)$items) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("Item") |>
    dplyr::transmute(
      Scale = sc,
      Item,
      a,
      Discrimination_categorization = dplyr::case_when(
        is.na(a) ~ NA_character_,
        a < 0    ~ "Negative (check keying/model)",
        a == 0   ~ "None",
        a < 0.35 ~ "Very Low",
        a < 0.65 ~ "Low",
        a < 1.35 ~ "Moderate",
        a < 1.70 ~ "High",
        TRUE     ~ "Very High"
      )
    )
  
  epq_irt_disc[[sc]] <- item_coef
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# Bind all outputs
# --------------------------------------------------
epq_irt_pars_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_pars)
epq_irt_tif_tbl  <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_tif)
epq_irt_fit_tbl  <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_fit)
epq_irt_rel_tbl  <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_rel)
epq_irt_conv_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_conv)
epq_irt_disc_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_disc)
epq_irt_m2_tbl   <- dplyr::bind_rows(epq_irt_m2)

# Add discrimination category into parameter table
epq_irt_pars_tbl <- epq_irt_pars_tbl |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    epq_irt_disc_tbl |>
      dplyr::select(Scale, Item, Discrimination_categorization),
    by = c("Scale", "Item")
  )

# Summary counts by scale
epq_irt_disc_summary <- epq_irt_disc_tbl |>
  dplyr::count(Scale, Discrimination_categorization, name = "n")

# Save files
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_conv_tbl, "outputs/tables/EPQ_IRT_convergence.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_rel_tbl,  "outputs/tables/EPQ_IRT_reliability.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_disc_tbl, "outputs/tables/EPQ_IRT_discrimination_by_item.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_disc_summary, "outputs/tables/EPQ_IRT_discrimination_summary.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_pars_tbl, "outputs/tables/epq_irt_pars_tbl.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_fit_tbl,  "outputs/tables/epq_irt_fit_tbl.csv")
readr::write_csv(epq_irt_m2_tbl,   "outputs/tables/EPQ_IRT_M2_fit.csv")
```

```{r IRTPlots, include=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# EPQ-R IRT Plots: TIC, IIC, ICC for all four subscales
# ============================================================



# --------------------------------------------------
# APA theme
# --------------------------------------------------
apatheme <- theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border     = element_rect(colour = "black"),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title       = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Subscale-specific colour palettes
# --------------------------------------------------
scale_fills <- c(
  Psychoticism = "#E64B35",
  Extraversion = "#4DBBD5",
  Neuroticism  = "#00A087",
  Lie          = "#F39B7F"
)

scale_colors <- c(
  Psychoticism = "#E64B35",
  Extraversion = "#4DBBD5",
  Neuroticism  = "#00A087",
  Lie          = "#F39B7F"
)

# item-level palettes (12 items each) using ggsci
item_palettes <- list(
  Psychoticism = colorRampPalette(c("#E64B35", "#B03021", "#FFB4A2"))(12),
  Extraversion = colorRampPalette(c("#4DBBD5", "#2980B9", "#AED6F1"))(12),
  Neuroticism  = colorRampPalette(c("#00A087", "#006D5B", "#A3D9CC"))(12),
  Lie          = colorRampPalette(c("#F39B7F", "#C0392B", "#FAD7A0"))(12)
)

theta_range <- seq(-4, 4, by = 0.01)


# ==========================================================
# 1. TEST INFORMATION CURVES (all four on one panel)
# ==========================================================
tic_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(epq_models), function(sc) {
  info <- testinfo(epq_models[[sc]], Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1))
  se   <- 1 / sqrt(info)
  data.frame(
    Theta       = theta_range,
    Information = as.numeric(info),
    SE          = as.numeric(se),
    Scale       = sc
  )
}))

tic_plot <- ggplot(tic_data, aes(x = Theta, y = Information, fill = Scale)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.35, position = "identity") +
  geom_line(aes(colour = Scale), linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = scale_fills) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = scale_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Test Information Curves: EPQ-RS-B Subscales",
    x     = expression(theta),
    y     = expression(I(theta))
  ) +
  apatheme +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_TIC_all_scales.png",
       tic_plot, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Optional: TIC with SE on secondary axis (per scale, saved individually)
for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  df_sc <- tic_data[tic_data$Scale == sc, ]
  se_scale <- max(df_sc$Information) / max(df_sc$SE)
  
  p <- ggplot(df_sc, aes(x = Theta)) +
    geom_area(aes(y = Information), fill = scale_fills[[sc]], alpha = 0.35) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Information), colour = scale_colors[[sc]], linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_line(aes(y = SE * se_scale), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      name = expression(I(theta)),
      sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / se_scale, name = expression(SE(theta)))
    ) +
    labs(title = paste0("TIC + SE: ", sc), x = expression(theta)) +
    apatheme
  
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_TIC_SE_", sc, ".png"),
         p, width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
}

# ==========================================================
# 2. ITEM INFORMATION CURVES (12 items per subscale panel)
# ==========================================================
iic_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  mod  <- epq_models[[sc]]
  nitems <- ncol(mod@Data$data)
  item_names <- colnames(mod@Data$data)
  
  iic_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nitems, function(i) {
    info <- iteminfo(extract.item(mod, i), Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1))
    data.frame(
      Theta       = theta_range,
      Information = as.numeric(info),
      Item        = item_names[i]
    )
  }))
  iic_df$Item <- factor(iic_df$Item, levels = item_names)
  
  iic_plots[[sc]] <- ggplot(iic_df, aes(x = Theta, y = Information)) +
    geom_area(fill = scale_fills[[sc]], alpha = 0.30) +
    geom_line(colour = scale_colors[[sc]], linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.20, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    facet_wrap(~ Item, ncol = 4) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Item Information Curves: ", sc),
      x     = expression(theta),
      y     = expression(I(theta))
    ) +
    apatheme +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8))
  
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_IIC_", sc, ".png"),
         iic_plots[[sc]], width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}

# ==========================================================
# 3. ITEM CHARACTERISTIC CURVES / ICC (trace plots, 12 per subscale)
# ==========================================================
icc_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  mod  <- epq_models[[sc]]
  nitems <- ncol(mod@Data$data)
  item_names <- colnames(mod@Data$data)
  
  icc_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nitems, function(i) {
    extr <- extract.item(mod, i)
    probs <- probtrace(extr, Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1))
    # For 2PL: column 2 = P(X=1)
    data.frame(
      Theta = theta_range,
      P     = probs[, 2],
      Item  = item_names[i]
    )
  }))
  icc_df$Item <- factor(icc_df$Item, levels = item_names)
  
  icc_plots[[sc]] <- ggplot(icc_df, aes(x = Theta, y = P)) +
    geom_line(colour = scale_colors[[sc]], linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    facet_wrap(~ Item, ncol = 4) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Item Characteristic Curves: ", sc),
      x     = expression(theta),
      y     = expression(P(theta))
    ) +
    apatheme +
    theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 8))
  
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_ICC_", sc, ".png"),
         icc_plots[[sc]], width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}

# ==========================================================
# 4. MARGINAL RELIABILITY CURVE (per subscale)
# ==========================================================
rel_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  mod <- epq_models[[sc]]
  info <- testinfo(mod, Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1))
  rel  <- as.numeric(info) / (1 + as.numeric(info))  # marginal reliability at each theta
  
  rel_df <- data.frame(Theta = theta_range, Reliability = rel, Scale = sc)
  
  rel_plots[[sc]] <- ggplot(rel_df, aes(x = Theta, y = Reliability)) +
    geom_area(fill = scale_fills[[sc]], alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_line(colour = scale_colors[[sc]], linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.70, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
    annotate("text", x = 3.2, y = 0.72, label = ".70", size = 3, colour = "red") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Marginal Reliability: ", sc),
      x     = expression(theta),
      y     = expression(rho(theta))
    ) +
    apatheme
  
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_MargRel_", sc, ".png"),
         rel_plots[[sc]], width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
}

# Combined marginal reliability
rel_all <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(epq_models), function(sc) {
  info <- testinfo(epq_models[[sc]], Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1))
  data.frame(Theta = theta_range, Reliability = as.numeric(info) / (1 + as.numeric(info)), Scale = sc)
}))

rel_combined <- ggplot(rel_all, aes(x = Theta, y = Reliability, colour = Scale)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.70, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = scale_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  labs(
    title = "Marginal Reliability Across Theta: EPQ-RS-B",
    x = expression(theta), y = expression(rho(theta))
  ) +
  apatheme + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_MargRel_combined.png",
       rel_combined, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# ==========================================================
# 5. EMPIRICAL RELIABILITY DENSITY (SE distribution)
# ==========================================================
emp_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  mod <- epq_models[[sc]]
  fs  <- fscores(mod, full.scores.SE = TRUE)
  
  emp_df <- data.frame(
    Theta = fs[, 1],
    SE    = fs[, 2],
    Scale = sc
  )
  
  emp_plots[[sc]] <- ggplot(emp_df, aes(x = Theta, y = SE)) +
    geom_point(colour = scale_colors[[sc]], alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6, se = FALSE) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = mean(emp_df$SE), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Empirical SE by Theta: ", sc),
      x     = expression(hat(theta)),
      y     = expression(SE(hat(theta)))
    ) +
    apatheme
  
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_EmpSE_", sc, ".png"),
         emp_plots[[sc]], width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)
}

# ==========================================================
# 6. COWPLOT COMPOSITES
# ==========================================================

# 6a. All four TIC + SE panels
tic_composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = lapply(names(epq_models), function(sc) {
    df_sc <- tic_data[tic_data$Scale == sc, ]
    se_scale <- max(df_sc$Information) / max(df_sc$SE)
    ggplot(df_sc, aes(x = Theta)) +
      geom_area(aes(y = Information), fill = scale_fills[[sc]], alpha = 0.35) +
      geom_line(aes(y = Information), colour = scale_colors[[sc]], linewidth = 0.7) +
      geom_line(aes(y = SE * se_scale), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
      scale_y_continuous(
        name = expression(I(theta)),
        sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / se_scale, name = expression(SE(theta)))
      ) +
      labs(title = sc, x = expression(theta)) +
      apatheme + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 10))
  }),
  ncol = 2, labels = "AUTO"
)
ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_TIC_composite.png",
       tic_composite, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# 6b. All IIC panels (one per subscale, stitched)
iic_composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = iic_plots, ncol = 1, labels = "AUTO"
)
ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_IIC_composite.png",
       iic_composite, width = 10, height = 30, dpi = 300)

# 6c. All ICC panels stitched
icc_composite <- plot_grid(
  plotlist = icc_plots, ncol = 1, labels = "AUTO"
)
ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_ICC_composite.png",
       icc_composite, width = 10, height = 30, dpi = 300)

# 6d. Marginal + Empirical SE side by side per subscale
for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  combo <- plot_grid(rel_plots[[sc]], emp_plots[[sc]], ncol = 2, labels = "AUTO")
  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_RelSE_combo_", sc, ".png"),
         combo, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
}

cat("All EPQ IRT plots saved to outputs/plots/\n")



# ==========================================================
# 7. PERSON-FIT PLOTS (Zh histograms, all four subscales)
# ==========================================================

epq_pf_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  pf <- mirt::personfit(epq_models[[sc]])
  
  misfits <- subset(pf, Zh < -2)
  cat(sc, ": ", nrow(misfits), " misfitting persons (Zh < -2)\n", sep = "")
  
  epq_pf_plots[[sc]] <- ggplot(pf, aes(x = Zh)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5, col = I("black"), fill = scale_fills[[sc]],
                   alpha = 0.6) +
    ylim(0, 200) + xlim(-4, 2) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = -2) +
    labs(y = "Number of Participants",
         x = paste0("Zh Statistics: ", sc)) +
    apatheme
}

epq_personfit_plot <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = epq_pf_plots,
  labels = "auto", ncol = 2, label_size = 20,
  align = "hv", label_fontface = "plain"
)

ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_personfit.png",
       epq_personfit_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)



# ==========================================================
# 8. PERSON-ITEM (WRIGHT) MAPS: EPQ-RS-B subscales (2PL)
# ==========================================================

epq_wright_plots <- list()

for (sc in names(epq_models)) {
  mod <- epq_models[[sc]]
  
  # Extract person theta estimates
  theta_scores <- fscores(mod, full.scores.SE = TRUE)[, 1]
  person_df <- data.frame(Theta = theta_scores)
  
  # Extract item difficulty (b) parameters
  pars <- coef(mod, simplify = TRUE)$items
  item_names <- rownames(pars)
  b_vals <- pars[, "d"]        # intercept in mirt parameterisation
  a_vals <- pars[, "a1"]
  # Convert mirt intercept (d) to traditional difficulty: b = -d/a
  b_traditional <- -b_vals / a_vals
  
  item_df <- data.frame(
    Item = item_names,
    Difficulty = b_traditional
  )
  item_df$Item <- factor(item_df$Item, levels = item_df$Item[order(item_df$Difficulty)])
  
  # Person distribution (left panel)
  p_person <- ggplot(person_df, aes(x = Theta)) +
    geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.3, fill = scale_fills[[sc]], alpha = 0.6,
                   col = I("black")) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-4, 4), breaks = seq(-4, 4, 1)) +
    labs(x = expression(theta), y = "Frequency",
         title = "Person Distribution") +
    apatheme

  # Item map (right panel)
  p_items <- ggplot(item_df, aes(x = Difficulty, y = Item)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, colour = scale_colors[[sc]]) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = min(person_df$Theta, item_df$Difficulty) - 0.5,
                     xend = Difficulty, y = Item, yend = Item),
                 linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey60") +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-4, 4), breaks = seq(-4, 4, 1)) +
    labs(x = expression(theta), y = NULL,
         title = "Item Difficulty (b)") +
    apatheme

  # Combine side by side
  epq_wright_plots[[sc]] <- cowplot::plot_grid(
    p_person, p_items,
    ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(0.4, 0.6),
    labels = paste0(sc), label_size = 12, label_fontface = "bold"
  )

  ggsave(paste0("outputs/plots/EPQ_WrightMap_", sc, ".png"),
         epq_wright_plots[[sc]], width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}

# Combined composite of all four Wright maps
epq_wright_composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = epq_wright_plots,
  ncol = 1, labels = "auto", label_size = 20,
  label_fontface = "plain"
)

ggsave("outputs/plots/EPQ_WrightMap_composite.png",
       epq_wright_composite, width = 12, height = 24, dpi = 300)
```


Across the 48 items, item fit was generally acceptable. All infit and out fit mean-square values fell within the range except for  one Lie scale item (EYP12).  RMSEA.S-X² values remained below .10 in all cases.
Item discrimination and difficulty parameters are reported in Table [X]. The four subscales differed markedly in the quality of their item parameters. Neuroticism exhibited the strongest and most homogeneous discrimination values. All 12 items achieved moderate to very high discrimination (a range = 1.15 to 3.08), with eight items classified as very high (a ≥ 1.70). Difficulty parameters were well distributed across the θ continuum, ranging from -1.78  to 1.33.

Extraversion items showed adequate discrimination overall (a range = 0.71 to 2.23), with three items classified as very high and three as high. Difficulty values ranged from -2.49  to 1.94, although difficulty values were somewhat concentrated below zero, suggesting that most items are relatively easy to endorse.

The Lie scale displayed a heterogeneous discrimination profile. Several items reached very high discrimination (EYP8, a = 2.02; EYP12, a = 2.47; EYP20, a = 1.71; EYP29, a = 1.80), but three items exhibited very low to low discrimination (EYP4, a = 0.29; EYP16, a = 0.61; EYP45, a = 0.59). Notably, EYP4 had an extreme difficulty value of -9.71, placing it far outside the observable θ range and rendering it essentially non-informative.

Psychoticism was the most problematic subscale. Discrimination values were highly heterogeneous, ranging from -0.07 (EYP2) to 1.76 (EYP18). EYP2 returned a negative discrimination estimate, indicating that endorsement probability decreased with increasing levels of the latent trait, a result that raises concerns about item keying or construct relevance in this sample. Two additional items (EYP31, a = 0.29; EYP6, a = 0.25) were classified as very low, contributing negligible measurement information. Difficulty parameters were predominantly high and positive (b range = 0.28 to 8.49 for positively discriminating items), indicating that most Psychoticism items are endorsed only at very elevated trait levels. EYP6 had an extreme difficulty value of 8.49, placing it well beyond the meaningful measurement range.


Marginal reliability coefficients were .83 for Neuroticism, .76 for Extraversion, .76 for the Lie scale, and .44 for Psychoticism. Empirical reliability estimates were .80, .70, .69, and -.22, respectively. The negative empirical reliability for Psychoticism indicates that the observed score variance attributable to measurement error exceeded the variance attributable to true score differences, rendering individual-level Psychoticism scores uninterpretable.

The TIC for Neuroticism peaked at 9.52 units of information (minimum SE = 0.32), and the scale maintained reliable measurement (marginal reliability ≥ .70) across a broad θ range from -1.55 to 1.66, spanning approximately 3.2 standard deviation units. Extraversion peaked at 5.26 (minimum SE = 0.44), with reliable measurement from θ = -1.99 to 0.99. The Lie scale peaked at 5.25 (minimum SE = 0.44), with reliable measurement from θ = -1.71 to 1.00. Psychoticism peaked at only 2.78 (minimum SE = 0.60) and achieved marginal reliability ≥ .70 only within the narrow range of θ = 1.65 to 2.87, a 1.2 standard deviation window located well above the population mean. This means that Psychoticism scores are reliable only for individuals approximately 1.5 to 3 standard deviations above the trait mean, a region containing a small fraction of any normative sample

### Person-Item Maps (Wright Maps)
Wright maps for the four EPQ-RS-B subscales are presented in Figure [X]. These maps juxtapose the distribution of person theta estimates (left panel) with item difficulty parameters (right panel) on a common metric, allowing visual evaluation of person-item targeting.
For Extraversion, the person distribution was approximately normal and centered near zero, with the bulk of respondents falling between θ = -2 and θ = 2. Item difficulties spanned a broad range from approximately -3 (EYP7) to 2 (EYP36), with reasonable coverage across the person distribution. Most items clustered between -1 and 1, overlapping well with the densest region of the person distribution. Two items (EYP3 and EYP36) had difficulty values above 1.5, providing some differentiation at the upper end of the trait continuum. Conversely, EYP7 and EYP32 anchored the lower end near -3 and -2 respectively. Overall, the Extraversion items demonstrated adequate targeting, though coverage was somewhat denser in the low-to-moderate range than at the positive extreme.
For Neuroticism, the person distribution was similarly centered near zero with a slight negative skew. Item difficulties ranged from approximately -2 (EYP34) to nearly 4 (EYP30). The majority of items fell between -1 and 1, covering the central region of the person distribution effectively. However, two items (EYP9 and EYP30) had difficulty values exceeding 3, placing them well beyond the upper boundary where most respondents were located. These items would only be endorsed by individuals with very high levels of neuroticism, and they contribute information almost exclusively at the positive extreme. Despite these outliers, the remaining ten items provided graduated coverage across the low-to-moderate trait range. The presence of EYP34 at roughly -2 ensured some measurement capacity at the lower end.
For the Lie scale, the person distribution was centered slightly below zero with most respondents falling between -2 and 1. Item difficulties spread from approximately -3 (EYP45) to nearly 4 (EYP47). Two items (EYP16 and EYP47) had difficulty values above 3, far exceeding the upper bound of the person distribution and therefore contributing negligible information for all but the most extreme scorers. At the opposite end, EYP45 and EYP40 had difficulties near -3 and -2, capturing variation among low scorers. The middle range was covered by a cluster of items between -1 and 0 (EYP8, EYP12, EYP37, EYP33). This pattern reveals a gap in item coverage between approximately 0 and 2.5, meaning the scale differentiates poorly among moderately high scorers on social desirability.
For Psychoticism, the person distribution was strongly negatively skewed, with the modal category near θ = -0.5 and a long positive tail. The overwhelming majority of respondents were concentrated between -1 and 0.5. In stark contrast, most item difficulties fell between 0 and 3, with EYP35 near 2.5 being the most difficult. Only two items (EYP14 at approximately -2 and EYP31 near -1) overlapped with the densest region of the person distribution. Three items (EYP2, EYP6, and EYP28) had no visible difficulty points on the plot, suggesting extremely high or computationally unstable difficulty estimates. This severe mismatch between person locations and item difficulties means the Psychoticism subscale provides almost no measurement precision for the majority of respondents, who score in the low-to-average range. The scale functions, in effect, as a set of very "hard" items that only differentiate among the small minority of individuals at the upper extreme of the trait.


```{r theta, include=FALSE}
# ==========================================================
# Find theta range where I(theta) >= SE(theta)
# ==========================================================
theta_range <- seq(-4, 4, by = 0.01)

epq_precision_range <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(epq_models), function(sc) {
  info <- as.numeric(testinfo(epq_models[[sc]], Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1)))
  se   <- 1 / sqrt(info)
  
  # Theta values where information exceeds SE
  precise <- theta_range[info >= se]
  
  data.frame(
    Scale     = sc,
    Theta_min = round(min(precise), 2),
    Theta_max = round(max(precise), 2),
    Peak_info = round(max(info), 3),
    Min_SE    = round(min(se), 3)
  )
}))

epq_precision_range
readr::write_csv(epq_precision_range, "outputs/tables/epq_precision_range.csv")

```

```{r marginaltehta, include=FALSE}
epq_reliable_range <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(epq_models), function(sc) {
  info <- as.numeric(testinfo(epq_models[[sc]], Theta = matrix(theta_range, ncol = 1)))
  rel  <- info / (1 + info)
  
  reliable <- theta_range[rel >= 0.70]
  
  data.frame(
    Scale     = sc,
    Theta_min = round(min(reliable), 2),
    Theta_max = round(max(reliable), 2),
    Peak_rel  = round(max(rel), 3),
    Info_at_70 = round(0.70 / (1 - 0.70), 3)  # = 2.333, for reference
  )
}))

epq_reliable_range
readr::write_csv(epq_reliable_range, "outputs/tables/epq_marginal_reliable_range.csv")

```



# Differential Item Functioning

```{r 2PLDIF, include=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# EPQ-RS DIF Analysis (Gender) — 2PL, no loops
# Four subscales tested independently using anchored baselines
# ============================================================

library(mirt)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(papaja)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 0. Settings
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ref_group <- "Female"
out_dir <- "outputs/DIF"
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ NA_character_,
    p < .001 ~ "< .001",
    TRUE     ~ sub("^0", "", sprintf("%.3f", p))
  )
}

fmt_num <- function(x, d = 2) {
  ifelse(is.na(x), "", sprintf(paste0("%.", d, "f"), x))
}

# optional: if your groups are coded differently, recode here
# demographics$gender <- factor(demographics$gender, levels = c("Female", "Male"))

# ============================================================
# 1. PSYCHOTICISM
# ============================================================

psy_scale <- "Psychoticism"
psy_items <- epq_item_sets[[psy_scale]]

psy_dat_raw <- as.data.frame(epq_items[, psy_items, drop = FALSE])
psy_grp_raw <- demographics$gender

psy_keep <- complete.cases(psy_dat_raw, psy_grp_raw)
psy_dat  <- psy_dat_raw[psy_keep, , drop = FALSE]
psy_grp  <- droplevels(factor(psy_grp_raw[psy_keep]))
if (ref_group %in% levels(psy_grp)) psy_grp <- relevel(psy_grp, ref = ref_group)
psy_dat[] <- lapply(psy_dat, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

psy_item_ok <- vapply(names(psy_dat), function(nm) {
  x <- psy_dat[[nm]]
  binary_ok <- all(na.omit(x) %in% c(0, 1))
  var_by_group <- tapply(x, psy_grp, function(z) length(unique(na.omit(z))) > 1)
  binary_ok && all(var_by_group)
}, logical(1))

psy_dat <- psy_dat[, psy_item_ok, drop = FALSE]

cat("\n--- Psychoticism ---\n")
cat("N =", nrow(psy_dat), "\n")
print(table(psy_grp))
cat("Items retained:", paste(names(psy_dat), collapse = ", "), "\n")

psy_base <- multipleGroup(
  data       = psy_dat,
  model      = 1,
  group      = psy_grp,
  itemtype   = "2PL",
  invariance = c("free_means", "free_var", colnames(psy_dat)),
  SE         = FALSE,
  verbose    = FALSE
)

psy_uniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = psy_base,
  which.par  = "d",
  items2test = colnames(psy_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

psy_nonuniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = psy_base,
  which.par  = "a1",
  items2test = colnames(psy_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

psy_both <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = psy_base,
  which.par  = c("a1", "d"),
  items2test = colnames(psy_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

psy_uniform_df <- as.data.frame(psy_uniform); psy_uniform_df$Item <- rownames(psy_uniform_df)
psy_nonuniform_df <- as.data.frame(psy_nonuniform); psy_nonuniform_df$Item <- rownames(psy_nonuniform_df)
psy_both_df <- as.data.frame(psy_both); psy_both_df$Item <- rownames(psy_both_df)

psy_uniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = psy_scale,
  Effect    = "Uniform DIF (d)",
  Item      = psy_uniform_df$Item,
  X2        = psy_uniform_df$X2,
  df        = psy_uniform_df$df,
  p         = psy_uniform_df$p,
  adj_p     = psy_uniform_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(psy_uniform_df$adj_p) & psy_uniform_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

psy_nonuniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = psy_scale,
  Effect    = "Nonuniform DIF (a1)",
  Item      = psy_nonuniform_df$Item,
  X2        = psy_nonuniform_df$X2,
  df        = psy_nonuniform_df$df,
  p         = psy_nonuniform_df$p,
  adj_p     = psy_nonuniform_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(psy_nonuniform_df$adj_p) & psy_nonuniform_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

psy_both_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = psy_scale,
  Effect    = "Combined DIF (a1 + d)",
  Item      = psy_both_df$Item,
  X2        = psy_both_df$X2,
  df        = psy_both_df$df,
  p         = psy_both_df$p,
  adj_p     = psy_both_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(psy_both_df$adj_p) & psy_both_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

psy_anchor_items <- psy_both_tbl %>% filter(DIF_flag == "No") %>% pull(Item)
psy_flagged_items <- psy_both_tbl %>% filter(DIF_flag == "Flagged") %>% pull(Item)

if (length(psy_anchor_items) < 2) stop("Psychoticism: too few anchor items.")

psy_anchor_model <- multipleGroup(
  data       = psy_dat,
  model      = 1,
  group      = psy_grp,
  itemtype   = "2PL",
  invariance = c("free_means", "free_var", psy_anchor_items),
  SE         = FALSE,
  verbose    = FALSE
)

psy_anchor_pars <- coef(psy_anchor_model, IRTpars = TRUE, simplify = TRUE)
psy_group_pars_tbl <- bind_rows(
  {
    x <- as.data.frame(psy_anchor_pars[[levels(psy_grp)[1]]]$items)
    x$Item <- rownames(x); x$Group <- levels(psy_grp)[1]; x$Scale <- psy_scale; x
  },
  {
    x <- as.data.frame(psy_anchor_pars[[levels(psy_grp)[2]]]$items)
    x$Item <- rownames(x); x$Group <- levels(psy_grp)[2]; x$Scale <- psy_scale; x
  }
)

psy_summary_tbl <- psy_both_tbl %>%
  summarise(
    Scale = psy_scale,
    n_items_tested = n(),
    n_flagged = sum(DIF_flag == "Flagged", na.rm = TRUE),
    flagged_items = ifelse(n_flagged == 0, "", paste(Item[DIF_flag == "Flagged"], collapse = ", "))
  )

# ============================================================
# 2. EXTRAVERSION
# ============================================================

ext_scale <- "Extraversion"
ext_items <- epq_item_sets[[ext_scale]]

ext_dat_raw <- as.data.frame(epq_items[, ext_items, drop = FALSE])
ext_grp_raw <- demographics$gender

ext_keep <- complete.cases(ext_dat_raw, ext_grp_raw)
ext_dat  <- ext_dat_raw[ext_keep, , drop = FALSE]
ext_grp  <- droplevels(factor(ext_grp_raw[ext_keep]))
if (ref_group %in% levels(ext_grp)) ext_grp <- relevel(ext_grp, ref = ref_group)
ext_dat[] <- lapply(ext_dat, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

ext_item_ok <- vapply(names(ext_dat), function(nm) {
  x <- ext_dat[[nm]]
  binary_ok <- all(na.omit(x) %in% c(0, 1))
  var_by_group <- tapply(x, ext_grp, function(z) length(unique(na.omit(z))) > 1)
  binary_ok && all(var_by_group)
}, logical(1))

ext_dat <- ext_dat[, ext_item_ok, drop = FALSE]

cat("\n--- Extraversion ---\n")
cat("N =", nrow(ext_dat), "\n")
print(table(ext_grp))
cat("Items retained:", paste(names(ext_dat), collapse = ", "), "\n")

ext_base <- multipleGroup(
  data       = ext_dat,
  model      = 1,
  group      = ext_grp,
  itemtype   = "2PL",
  invariance = c("free_means", "free_var", colnames(ext_dat)),
  SE         = FALSE,
  verbose    = FALSE
)

ext_uniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = ext_base,
  which.par  = "d",
  items2test = colnames(ext_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

ext_nonuniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = ext_base,
  which.par  = "a1",
  items2test = colnames(ext_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

ext_both <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = ext_base,
  which.par  = c("a1", "d"),
  items2test = colnames(ext_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

ext_uniform_df <- as.data.frame(ext_uniform); ext_uniform_df$Item <- rownames(ext_uniform_df)
ext_nonuniform_df <- as.data.frame(ext_nonuniform); ext_nonuniform_df$Item <- rownames(ext_nonuniform_df)
ext_both_df <- as.data.frame(ext_both); ext_both_df$Item <- rownames(ext_both_df)

ext_uniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = ext_scale,
  Effect    = "Uniform DIF (d)",
  Item      = ext_uniform_df$Item,
  X2        = ext_uniform_df$X2,
  df        = ext_uniform_df$df,
  p         = ext_uniform_df$p,
  adj_p     = ext_uniform_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(ext_uniform_df$adj_p) & ext_uniform_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

ext_nonuniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = ext_scale,
  Effect    = "Nonuniform DIF (a1)",
  Item      = ext_nonuniform_df$Item,
  X2        = ext_nonuniform_df$X2,
  df        = ext_nonuniform_df$df,
  p         = ext_nonuniform_df$p,
  adj_p     = ext_nonuniform_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(ext_nonuniform_df$adj_p) & ext_nonuniform_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

ext_both_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = ext_scale,
  Effect    = "Combined DIF (a1 + d)",
  Item      = ext_both_df$Item,
  X2        = ext_both_df$X2,
  df        = ext_both_df$df,
  p         = ext_both_df$p,
  adj_p     = ext_both_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(ext_both_df$adj_p) & ext_both_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

ext_anchor_items <- ext_both_tbl %>% filter(DIF_flag == "No") %>% pull(Item)
ext_flagged_items <- ext_both_tbl %>% filter(DIF_flag == "Flagged") %>% pull(Item)

if (length(ext_anchor_items) < 2) stop("Extraversion: too few anchor items.")

ext_anchor_model <- multipleGroup(
  data       = ext_dat,
  model      = 1,
  group      = ext_grp,
  itemtype   = "2PL",
  invariance = c("free_means", "free_var", ext_anchor_items),
  SE         = FALSE,
  verbose    = FALSE
)

ext_anchor_pars <- coef(ext_anchor_model, IRTpars = TRUE, simplify = TRUE)
ext_group_pars_tbl <- bind_rows(
  {
    x <- as.data.frame(ext_anchor_pars[[levels(ext_grp)[1]]]$items)
    x$Item <- rownames(x); x$Group <- levels(ext_grp)[1]; x$Scale <- ext_scale; x
  },
  {
    x <- as.data.frame(ext_anchor_pars[[levels(ext_grp)[2]]]$items)
    x$Item <- rownames(x); x$Group <- levels(ext_grp)[2]; x$Scale <- ext_scale; x
  }
)

ext_summary_tbl <- ext_both_tbl %>%
  summarise(
    Scale = ext_scale,
    n_items_tested = n(),
    n_flagged = sum(DIF_flag == "Flagged", na.rm = TRUE),
    flagged_items = ifelse(n_flagged == 0, "", paste(Item[DIF_flag == "Flagged"], collapse = ", "))
  )

# ============================================================
# 3. NEUROTICISM
# ============================================================

neu_scale <- "Neuroticism"
neu_items <- epq_item_sets[[neu_scale]]

neu_dat_raw <- as.data.frame(epq_items[, neu_items, drop = FALSE])
neu_grp_raw <- demographics$gender

neu_keep <- complete.cases(neu_dat_raw, neu_grp_raw)
neu_dat  <- neu_dat_raw[neu_keep, , drop = FALSE]
neu_grp  <- droplevels(factor(neu_grp_raw[neu_keep]))
if (ref_group %in% levels(neu_grp)) neu_grp <- relevel(neu_grp, ref = ref_group)
neu_dat[] <- lapply(neu_dat, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))

neu_item_ok <- vapply(names(neu_dat), function(nm) {
  x <- neu_dat[[nm]]
  binary_ok <- all(na.omit(x) %in% c(0, 1))
  var_by_group <- tapply(x, neu_grp, function(z) length(unique(na.omit(z))) > 1)
  binary_ok && all(var_by_group)
}, logical(1))

neu_dat <- neu_dat[, neu_item_ok, drop = FALSE]

cat("\n--- Neuroticism ---\n")
cat("N =", nrow(neu_dat), "\n")
print(table(neu_grp))
cat("Items retained:", paste(names(neu_dat), collapse = ", "), "\n")

neu_base <- multipleGroup(
  data       = neu_dat,
  model      = 1,
  group      = neu_grp,
  itemtype   = "2PL",
  invariance = c("free_means", "free_var", colnames(neu_dat)),
  SE         = FALSE,
  verbose    = FALSE
)

neu_uniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = neu_base,
  which.par  = "d",
  items2test = colnames(neu_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

neu_nonuniform <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = neu_base,
  which.par  = "a1",
  items2test = colnames(neu_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

neu_both <- DIF(
  MGmodel    = neu_base,
  which.par  = c("a1", "d"),
  items2test = colnames(neu_dat),
  scheme     = "drop",
  simplify   = TRUE,
  p.adjust   = "BH",
  verbose    = FALSE
)

neu_uniform_df <- as.data.frame(neu_uniform); neu_uniform_df$Item <- rownames(neu_uniform_df)
neu_nonuniform_df <- as.data.frame(neu_nonuniform); neu_nonuniform_df$Item <- rownames(neu_nonuniform_df)
neu_both_df <- as.data.frame(neu_both); neu_both_df$Item <- rownames(neu_both_df)

neu_uniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = neu_scale,
  Effect    = "Uniform DIF (d)",
  Item      = neu_uniform_df$Item,
  X2        = neu_uniform_df$X2,
  df        = neu_uniform_df$df,
  p         = neu_uniform_df$p,
  adj_p     = neu_uniform_df$adj_p,
  DIF_flag  = ifelse(!is.na(neu_uniform_df$adj_p) & neu_uniform_df$adj_p < .05, "Flagged", "No")
)

neu_nonuniform_tbl <- tibble(
  Scale     = neu_scale,
  Effect    = "Nonuniform DIF (a1)",
```
